patch.value:
## Context
The SDK (src/sdk) currently serves extension/package AUTHORS (compose/define/runtime/testing/cli-contracts). The PM primitives themselves — item store, list/query, search, history, governance, schema/config/profile — live in src/core and are imported directly by src/cli/commands/*. Integrators who want a custom PM tool must shell out to the CLI; the product goal says the SDK should deliver the universal baseline.

## Decision (proposed)
1. src/sdk is the ONLY public programmatic API; everything in src/core is private implementation.
2. src/cli and src/mcp may import ONLY from src/sdk (and their own presentation modules). Enforced by lint boundary rule + CI gate.
3. A primitive enters the SDK with: typed input/result contracts, docstring coverage, linked tests, and reference docs — same bar as existing sdk/testing helpers.
4. SDK surface is semver-meaningful: breaking changes to promoted primitives require a deprecation cycle.
5. MCP tools and CLI commands map 1:1 onto SDK primitives wherever a primitive exists (no logic forks in presentation layers).

## Alternatives considered
- Status quo (CLI imports core): rejected — SDK can never reach feature parity; universality goal unreachable.
- Export src/core wholesale: rejected — freezes internals, no stability contract, huge accidental API.
- Separate @unbrained/pm-core package: rejected for now — split repos/version skew; can revisit after boundary is stable.

## Consequences
+ CLI/MCP/SDK share one implementation — feature drift becomes impossible.
+ Any project can compose its own PM tool from primitives (universality goal).
- Promotion work is a wide mechanical refactor (mitigated by domain-by-domain child tasks under pm-usfg and a zero-behavior-change test bar).
ADR: the pm SDK is the single public API — CLI and MCP are presentation layers (proposed)

Fixes the layering contract for the SDK-first epic pm-usfg: src/core is private, src/sdk is the only public programmatic surface, src/cli and src/mcp import exclusively from src/sdk, enforced by a CI boundary gate. Status open = proposed; close = accepted. Duplicate check: no existing ADR covers public-API layering.

ADR body records context, layering rules, considered alternatives (status quo core-direct imports; publish core as-is; separate npm package for primitives), promotion criteria for a primitive to enter the SDK, semver/stability policy for the SDK surface, and the boundary-gate enforcement mechanism; accepted (closed) before promotion tasks under pm-usfg merge

author: claude-code-agent

patch.value:
## Context
The SDK (src/sdk) currently serves extension/package AUTHORS (compose/define/runtime/testing/cli-contracts). The PM primitives themselves — item store, list/query, search, history, governance, schema/config/profile — live in src/core and are still imported directly by parts of src/cli and src/mcp. Integrators who want a custom PM tool must shell out to the CLI; the product goal says the SDK should deliver the universal baseline.

## Decision (proposed)
1. Target architecture: src/sdk is the public programmatic API; src/core remains private implementation.
2. Target presentation boundary: src/cli and src/mcp become thin presentation layers over src/sdk primitives as each primitive family is promoted.
3. Current enforcement is a ratchet, not a completed SDK-only whitelist: CI records the existing src/cli/src/mcp -> src/core edges in scripts/release/sdk-import-boundary-baseline.json and blocks new private core edges, computed dynamic imports from boundary sources, and stale baseline entries.
4. A primitive enters the SDK with: typed input/result contracts, docstring coverage, linked tests, and reference docs — same bar as existing sdk/testing helpers.
5. SDK surface is semver-meaningful: breaking changes to promoted primitives require a deprecation cycle.
6. MCP tools and CLI commands map 1:1 onto SDK primitives wherever a primitive exists (no logic forks in presentation layers).

## Alternatives considered
- Status quo (CLI imports core): rejected — SDK can never reach feature parity; universality goal unreachable.
- Export src/core wholesale: rejected — freezes internals, no stability contract, huge accidental API.
- Separate @unbrained/pm-core package: rejected for now — split repos/version skew; can revisit after boundary is stable.

## Consequences
+ CLI/MCP/SDK converge on one implementation as the baseline shrinks.
+ Any project can compose its own PM tool from primitives (universality goal).
+ The ratchet is enforceable before the migration is complete.
- Promotion work is a wide mechanical refactor (mitigated by domain-by-domain child tasks under pm-usfg and a zero-behavior-change test bar).

ADR body records context, target layering rules, current ratchet enforcement, considered alternatives (status quo core-direct imports; publish core as-is; separate npm package for primitives), promotion criteria for a primitive to enter the SDK, semver/stability policy for the SDK surface, and the boundary-gate mechanism; accepted (closed) before promotion tasks under pm-usfg merge

Target layering for SDK-first pm: src/core is private, src/sdk is the public programmatic surface, and CLI/MCP should become SDK presentation layers. Current CI enforcement is a baseline ratchet: no new private src/core imports, no computed dynamic imports from boundary sources, and no stale baseline entries while promotion tasks burn down existing edges. Status open = proposed; close = accepted.

message: Align ADR wording with current SDK boundary ratchet enforcement

patch.value: Accepted ADR before SDK primitive promotion: live item body records target layering, rejected alternatives, promotion criteria, semver policy, and boundary-gate ratchet; pm-xzhz now implements the first public execution primitive against that decision.
